title: Sinarundinaria × tranquillans
taxonomy: Sinarundinaria × tranquillans is classified as a hybrid bamboo species within the Poaceae family. It belongs to the genus Sinarundinaria, which is native to Asia. Specifically, it is classified as Sinarundinaria × tranquillans within the section Arundinariaeae. This hybrid bamboo species is known for its distinct characteristics and growth habits. Further classification may be available in specific botanical or horticultural resources.
uses: Sinarundinaria × tranquillans, also known as Zebra grass, has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and stems are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, it is used to treat fever, sore throat, and other respiratory issues. The plant's leaves are also used to make tea, which is said to have a calming effect and help with digestion. However, its medicinal uses and efficacy require further scientific research and validation.

intro: Sinarundinaria × tranquillans, also known as Mountain Bamboo, is a hybrid bamboo species native to East Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat. The bioactive constituents of Mountain Bamboo include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Mountain Bamboo include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote recovery and well-being.
